text: The hilarious part is that if he'd re-signed with Toronto, the Raptors stand a very good shot of having 2-3 more rings right now, and the S&C staff and load management mean Kawhi is probably injured a LOT less than he has been. They probably never sell in the retool, never lose Masai Uriji, and keep Annouby and the others. Instead, Kawhi has had no success, and has basically been a footnote in the league since 2020.
label: analysis

text: Sorry, but you can't retroactively call the kawhi signing or the PG trade bad with the benefit of hindsight. You can only judge it by what we knew at the time. PG was PG, and he was balling out at the time, and nobody really knew what SGA would turn into.
label: analysis

text: The franchise absolutely did not deserve better. They knew Kawhi had a degenerative knee condition, which has been the cause of a lot of the issues the team has had once they signed him. It was the biggest story in the NBA for an entire fucking year. There was no mystery there. They still happily signed him, made a trade that convinced him to sign, and later signed him to an extension.
label: analysis

text: He was a terrible signing not just because of what he's done (or not done), but also his demand to have Paul George on the team with him. The Clippers had to give up a bunch of future picks to get Paul George, who played a max of 56 games a season for the Clippers.
label: analysis

text: All of this rings true. But no, we're not going to pretend Ballmer has not done this to himself. Kawhi hasn't abused an organization, the organization bent broke and circumvented every rule to get its way. It didn't work out, so now Kawhi is the perpetrator and Ballmer is the victim.
label: analysis

text: Other than 2 seasons the 5 seemed like normal seasons for him. They knew load management was his spirit animal before they signed him. He finished top 10 in MVP voting twice and 13th another time. Also, 4 all-star games in those 5 seasons.
